qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Deeply nested XML causes stack exhaustion in DOMParser.parseFromString
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding reaches `Document.normalize()` -> recursive `Element.normalize()` in `lib/dom.js`, which the advisory explicitly lists as an affected traversal sharing the same missing depth guard/root cause.

selected_finding.title: Deeply nested XML causes stack exhaustion in DOMParser.parseFromString

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding identifies the advisory's recursive `normalize()` root cause in `lib/dom.js`, and a fresh copied-workspace PoC run reproduced the stack-exhaustion denial of service on a deeply nested XML payload.
evidence: The advisory details explicitly list `Node.prototype.normalize()` in `lib/dom.js` as an affected recursive traversal with no depth guard, and note that it was reported via `DOMParser.parseFromString()`.

The finding's exploit path is `DOMParser.parseFromString -> DOMHandler.endDocument -> Document.normalize -> recursive child.normalize -> RangeError`, which is the same vulnerable traversal family described by the advisory.

Fresh execution from `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_38fa38bf/.judge/poc_runs/domparser_deep_nesting_stack_overflow/finding_copy` exited successfully and wrote runtime artifacts under `.judge/poc_runs/domparser_deep_nesting_stack_overflow`.

The run log shows a control parse succeeded, then a 20,000-level payload triggered `RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded` with repeated `Element.normalize` frames in `/target-repo/lib/dom.js`, satisfying the advisory-specific denial-of-service success condition.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC parsed a control document successfully, then a 20,000-level XML payload caused `RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded` with repeated `Element.normalize` frames from `/target-repo/lib/dom.js`, proving stack-exhaustion denial of service in the advisory-listed recursive normalize path during `DOMParser.parseFromString`.
